title: techmap-ing module with underspecified input port connections creates new undriven signal
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

```
read_verilog <<EOT
module sub(input i, output o, input j);
foobar f(i, o, j);
endmodule
EOT
design -stash techmap

read_verilog <<EOT
(* blackbox *)
module sub(input i, output o, input j);
endmodule

(* blackbox *)
module foobar(input i, output o, input j);
endmodule

module top(input i, output o);
sub s0(i, o);
endmodule
EOT

### techmap -map %techmap
hierarchy -check
check -assert
```
Remove `###` to error out on `check -assert`

## Expected behavior

Identical behaviour as with `techmap` commented out -- no error.

## Actual behavior

```
5. Executing CHECK pass (checking for obvious problems).
checking module top..
Warning: Wire top.\s0.j is used but has no driver.
found and reported 1 problems.
```
where resulting netlist is:
```
module \top
  attribute \src "<<EOT:9"
  wire input 1 \i
  attribute \src "<<EOT:9"
  wire output 2 \o
  attribute \src "<<EOT:10|<<EOT:1"
  wire \s0.i
  attribute \src "<<EOT:10|<<EOT:1"
  wire \s0.j
  attribute \src "<<EOT:10|<<EOT:1"
  wire \s0.o
  attribute \module_not_derived 1
  attribute \src "<<EOT:10|<<EOT:2"
  cell \foobar \s0.f
    connect \i \i
    connect \j \s0.j
    connect \o \o
  end
end
```
code_blocks:
5. Executing CHECK pass (checking for obvious problems).
checking module top..
Warning: Wire top.\s0.j is used but has no driver.
found and reported 1 problems.

module \top
  attribute \src "<<EOT:9"
  wire input 1 \i
  attribute \src "<<EOT:9"
  wire output 2 \o
  attribute \src "<<EOT:10|<<EOT:1"
  wire \s0.i
  attribute \src "<<EOT:10|<<EOT:1"
  wire \s0.j
  attribute \src "<<EOT:10|<<EOT:1"
  wire \s0.o
  attribute \module_not_derived 1
  attribute \src "<<EOT:10|<<EOT:2"
  cell \foobar \s0.f
    connect \i \i
    connect \j \s0.j
    connect \o \o
  end
end
